task: T8f density-controlled Conway sweep on 20x20 grid (T8e hypothesis test at larger grid)

comparison_with_10x10_t8e.shift_finding: Sweet spot density SHIFTS UP by grid size — 10x10 (small): peak at 16%; 20x20 (large): peak at 32-50%

key_finding: Density alone NOT governing — sweet spot density SHIFTS UP with larger grid. 10x10 peak at 16% (16 cells); 20x20 peak at 32% (128 cells) or 50% (200 cells). Larger grid requires HIGHER density to reach Markov-incapable chaos. Total live cells alone is NOT invariant either (16 vs 128-200). The actual governing variable is likely multi-dimensional state-space-vs-density combination.
absolute_count_attempt: 10x10 peak: 16 cells; 20x20 peak: 128-200 cells. NOT invariant 'total cells' — 8-12x difference between sweet spot total counts.

10x10 16% = 16 cells gives same +30 as R-pentomino chaotic — independent confirmation
20x20 16% = 64 cells gives only +8.5 — might be train-volume bottleneck, not pure density issue
law_64_status_post_t8f: Multi-dimensional re-statement further refined: 'CA(5) advantage is grid-size × density × pattern-structure × train-volume conditional.
